title: Second Battle of El Alamein
summary: The Second Battle of El Alamein was the decisive engagement in the North African campaign. Under General Montgomery, the British 8th Army launched a set-piece offensive that shattered Rommel's Panzerarmee Afrika and began a pursuit that would not end until Tunisia.

commanders: Georg Stumme (died of heart attack 24 Oct)

outcome: Decisive Allied victory. Rommel's army routed; retreat all the way to Tunisia. Hitler famously ordered the Panzerarmee to 'victory or death' and not to retreat — Rommel disobeyed and saved the remnant of his force.
significance: Churchill declared: 'Before Alamein we never had a victory. After Alamein we never had a defeat.' The battle marked the end of Axis ambitions in North Africa.
